title: Customizable Number Formatting

posts.author: gfolli (gfolli)
posts.cookedHtml:
I hope this message finds you well.

I would like to request a feature implementation regarding number formatting within Monday.com. Currently, I use Google Sheets to manage my data before importing it into Monday.com. In Google Sheets, I follow the Brazilian numeric format, where a period (.) separates thousands and a comma (,) denotes decimals (e.g., 1.000,35). However, Monday.com only supports the U.S. format, where a comma (,) separates thousands and a period (.) represents decimals (e.g., 1,000.35).

This inconsistency leads to issues during data import, causing incorrect values and requiring manual adjustments, which is both time-consuming and prone to errors.

Would it be possible to implement a setting that allows users to customize number formatting based on their regional preferences? This would greatly improve accuracy and usability for international users.

I appreciate your time and consideration and look forward to your response.

Best regards,

GFolli

posts.author: popjaco (Poppy)
posts.cookedHtml: I recommend you put your vote onto this feature request, which appears to be asking for the same thing? Number column: Add options for decimal places and number formats
